item.name: Clusters

description.content: Manage Databricks clusters for running Apache Spark workloads. Create, start, restart, resize, terminate, and permanently delete clusters.

item.name: Azure Databricks Create a New Cluster

request.name: Azure Databricks Create a New Cluster

description.content: Creates a new Apache Spark cluster. Returns the ID of the newly created cluster. The cluster starts in a PENDING state and transitions to RUNNING when ready. Optionally, you can attach libraries to the cluster after creation.

variable.description: The unique identifier for your Azure Databricks workspace instance. Found in the workspace URL.

response.name: Cluster created successfully

item.name: Azure Databricks Submit a One-time Run

request.name: Azure Databricks Submit a One-time Run

The unique identifier for your Azure Databricks workspace instance. Found in the workspace URL.

Core REST API for managing Azure Databricks workspaces, clusters, jobs, and workspace objects programmatically. This specification covers the Clusters API (2.0), Jobs API (2.1), and Workspace API (2.0) endpoints available on Azure Databricks instances.

Contact Support:
 Name: Azure Databricks Support
